data.title: OSI Systems receives $4M award for patient monitoring solutions
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) on Wednesday said its Healthcare division, Spacelabs Healthcare, is expanding its footprint with an existing U.S.-based customer under an agreement to provide approximately $4 million of patient monitoring solutions and related supplies and accessories to the hospital.
  • OSIS -0.7% premarket to $193.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allakos surges on reverse merger deal with Concentra Biosciences
data.description:
• Nanocap biotech Allakos (NASDAQ:ALLK) announced a merger agreement on Wednesday to be acquired by privately held Concentra Biosciences, LLC, for $0.33 per share in cash as part of a deal expected to close in May.
  • Shares of San Carlos, California-based biotech climbed ~46% after the announcement. 
  • Per the terms, a subsidiary of Concentra is scheduled to begin a tender offer by Apr. 15 to buy all outstanding shares of Allakos (NASDAQ:ALLK) common stock.
  • “Allakos’ Board of Directors has unanimously determined that the acquisition by Concentra is in the best interests of all Allakos shareholders,” the company said.
  • ALLK added that its officers, directors, and their related parties, representing nearly 8% of the company’s common stock, have signed agreements in favor of the deal and pledged to tender their shares when the offer begins.

data.title: ConnectM receives buyout offer from its institutional investors
data.description:
• ConnectM Technology (NASDAQ:CNTM) said on Wednesday that it has received a non-binding proposal offering $1.60 per share in cash from its three largest institutional investors—SriSid, Arumilli, and Win-Light Global —to acquire all remaining outstanding shares of the company and transition ConnectM into a privately held entity.
  • The offer, submitted on March 31, 2025, implies a total equity valuation of approximately $46.5M for ConnectM.
  • SriSid, Arumilli, and Win-Light Global have been long-term investors in ConnectM, having initially invested in the company in 2020.

data.title: Stock index futures drop as Trump's tariffs take center stage; private sector job growth surges
data.description:
Stock index futures were in the red on Wednesday, as investors braced for President Trump's reciprocal tariff plans, while U.S. private sector job growth surged in March.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.6%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.18%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.89%.

March U.S. private sector employment came in at +155K vs. +120K consensus and +84K prior (revised from +77K), according to the ADP National Employment Report released on Wednesday.

Wall Street's major averages finished mixed Tuesday in the jumpstart of Q2 action, as investors waited for the Trump administration's “Liberation Day” plan to impose large-scale tariffs on trading partners worldwide.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday is slated to formally announce "country-based" tariffs during an appearance in the Rose Garden at 4 pm ET.

"We clearly don’t know any of the details, including which countries will be targeted and at what rate, with reporting yesterday suggesting that a final decision was still to be made," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The U.S. Trade Representative's office is preparing a third tier of tariffs that would be lower than a potential 20% universal levy option, The Wall Street Journal reported Tuesday.

Ahead of today’s announcement, fears about stagflation in financial markets continued to mount, even if markets had a pretty positive day yesterday. The stagflation fears were exacerbated by the latest batch of U.S. data, Reid added. 

"The erratic nature of trade policy, uncertainty about whether legal procedures will be followed, limited clarity about end-objectives, the possibility that Trump will again retreat from some taxes, and the unpredictability of patronage-style deals to gain exemptions create uncertainty about the direct effect of the taxes," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The second-round effects depend on unpredictable reactions by U.S. companies, a far more complex global trade system than existed 20 years ago, and U.S. consumers whose reaction to inflation has shifted, Donovan added. 

The economic calendar is on the lighter side today, with mainly February factory orders slated to land, which is expected to come in at 0.5% M/M.

data.title: Domino's Pizza partners with DoorDash
data.description:
To expand its exposure in the food delivery industry, Domino’s Pizza (NASDAQ:DPZ)  announced a collaboration with DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) which would enable customers to order Domino’s pizza through the DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) Marketplace app with delivery completed by Domino’s (NASDAQ:DPZ) drivers.

"The ability to connect seamlessly with DoorDash customers means more sales for Domino's stores, while efficiently leveraging our brand's robust delivery network. Tapping into incremental customers, particularly in suburban and rural markets, is a meaningful opportunity for Domino's, as our brand continues to open stores nationwide," said Domino’s COO Joe Jordan.

The announcement with DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) comes as little surprise as Domino’s CEO Russell Weiner anticipated further partnerships in the aggregator channel and was already in negotiations with third parties, targeting a $1B incremental sales opportunity.

“The aggregator marketplace is the fastest growing segment within QSR Pizza, and we are just getting started,” Weiner said on the company’s Q4 earnings call with analysts

Domino’s (NASDAQ:DPZ) had a similar agreement with Uber Eats (UBER) beginning in July 2023 in which U.S. customers could order delivery from their local Domino’s store menu with Uber Eats (UBER) as the exclusive third-party platform. By taking orders through the Uber Eats platform, Domino’s gained access to a new segment of customers. This exclusivity agreement was initially intended to run through the end of 2024 but has been extended until May 1. According to Domino’s CEO Russell Weiner, the collaboration with Uber (UBER) contributed 3% to U.S. sales in 2024.

data.title: Snowflake started with Neutral rating at Macquarie
data.description:
Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW) was in focus on Wednesday after investment firm Macquarie started coverage on the data warehousing company with a Neutral rating.

"We like the company's core cloud data warehouse opportunity and momentum, market expansion opportunities and disciplined execution," analyst Steve Koenig wrote in a note to clients. "We see some risks from more aggressive FY26 product revenueguidance, competitors' AI positioning, and macro pressures on consumption."

Koenig said the positives and negatives to investing in Snowflake are "roughly balanced," with the company disrupting and gaining share in the $80B database management system market. It has also expanded its total addressable market through new use cases in data engineering and artificial intelligence, and it has a high "Rule of 40" score that reiterates the strength of its market position, product differentiation and sales execution.

On the flip side, there are some risks that Snowflake is not as well positioned as the hyperscalers in capitalizing on the generative AI opportunities, Koenig added. He also pointed to competition with Databricks heating up, its initial guidance for fiscal 2026 product revenue leaves less room for upside than its 2025 guidance, and consumption-based revenue could see pressure, especially if the broader economy slows down.

data.title: UniFirst beats bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• UniFirst press release (NYSE:UNF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.31 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $602.22M (+1.9% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased to $68.9 million compared to $64.8 million in the prior year, or 6.3%.
  • The quarterly tax rate decreased to 25.0% compared to 26.2% in the prior year.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and Short-term investments totaled $201.0 million as of March 1, 2025.
  • The Company had no long-term debt outstanding as of March 1, 2025.
  • Cash flow from operating activities was $128.3 million in the first half of fiscal 2025, an increase of 20.2% over the prior year.

  • Financial Outlook

    Mr. Sintros continued, “At this time, we expect our revenues for fiscal 2025 to be between $2.422 billion and $2.432 billion [vs. $2.43B consensus (prior $2.425 billion and $2.440 billion)] which reflects the anticipated negative impact of the Canadian Dollar exchange rate compared to our original expectations. We further expect that our diluted earnings per share will be between $7.30 and $7.70 [vs. $7.17 consensus (prior $6.79 and $7.19)], which reflects improved anticipated operating income in our Core Laundry Operations and an assumption that our Key Initiative costs in fiscal 2025 will approximate $12.0 million, revised down from prior estimates.”

data.title: Leggett & Platt plans $285M sale of aerospace products group
data.description:
Leggett & Platt (NYSE:LEG) reached an agreement to sell its Aerospace Products Group to investment funds managed by Tinicum Inc. in a deal valued at $285 million, the company announced. The sale, which is pending regulatory approval and other closing conditions, is expected to be finalized in 2025, according to an announcement on Wednesday.

After accounting for taxes, the company anticipates net cash proceeds of around $240 million from the transaction.

The Aerospace Products Group designs and manufactures complex tube and duct assemblies primarily for commercial and military aircraft, as well as space launch vehicles. The unit operates seven production facilities in the United States, the United Kingdom and France, and employs approximately 700 people. It generated $190 million in net trade sales in 2024.

The move comes as part of Leggett & Platt’s (NYSE:LEG) ongoing strategic review, which aims to align its portfolio with long-term priorities. The company plans to issue updated full-year guidance for 2025 after the sale is completed and the aerospace unit is excluded from future financial reporting.

data.title: Leggett & Platt sells its Aerospace Products Group for $285M
data.description:
• Leggett & Platt (NYSE:LEG) said on Wednesday that it has signed an agreement to sell its Aerospace Products Group to affiliated funds managed by Tinicum Incorporated for a cash purchase price of $285M.
  • The after-tax cash proceeds are expected to be approximately $240M.
  • The Aerospace Products Group is a supplier of complex, highly engineered tube and duct assemblies for use primarily in commercial and military aircraft platforms and space launch vehicles.
  • The business comprises seven manufacturing facilities located in the U.S., UK, and France and approximately 700 employees with net trade sales of $190M in 2024.
  • This divestiture is part of the ongoing strategic business review, aimed at determining which businesses are the right long-term fit for the company.
  • Leggett & Platt (LEG) shares were up 0.51% in premarket trading.

data.title: Petco rallies after its CEO scoops up more shares
data.description:
Petco Health and Wellness Company (NASDAQ:WOOF) rallied in early trading on Wednesday after CEO Joel Anderson picked up more shares in the pet retailer.

In an SEC filing, Petco (NASDAQ:WOOF) disclosed that Anderson purchased 1,586,088 shares in a range of $2.91 to $3.03 for a total amount of more than $4.7 million. Anderson assumed the role of CEO on July 29, 2024, and also serves on Petco's (WOOF) board of directors. Before joining Petco (WOOF), Anderson served as CEO of Five Below (FIVE), where he oversaw significant growth, expanding the company from 366 stores to over 1,500 locations and increasing revenue from $500 million to over $3.5 billion.

With its Q4 earnings report, Petco (WOOF) reported revenue fell 7.3% year-over-year to $1.6 billion, while comparable sales were up 0.5%. Adjusted EBITDA was $96.1 million vs. $105.3 million a year ago. EPS was reported at -$0.05 vs. -$0.02 consensus and -$0.08 a year ago. 

Shares of Petco (WOOF) were up 6.1% in premarket action to $3.14 vs. the 52-week range of $1.41 to $6.29. Short interest on WOOF stands at 11.8% of the total float.
